decision.reason: The GCP adapter has contract render and smoke execution evidence, generates a deterministic Google Workflows source plan that orders multi-contract project jobs, polls submitted BigQuery jobs until DONE with a bounded timeout, includes connector retry blocks, emits execution-plan, broad/execution-scoped evidence-readback and cleanup-plan artifacts, exposes adapter-owned deploy/run/wait/readback/reset/cleanup flags, and has live evidence for generated-runner deploy/execute, command-path readback, runner-side run/quality/schema evidence, quality failed-row semantics, execution-scoped evidence ids, workflow cleanup, write-failure evidence, target/evidence cleanup and repeated full-project rerun execution/readback. Google Workflows is now the certified GCP project deployment runner.
decision.planner_behavior: Treat the generated Google Workflows runner as the promoted GCP deployment orchestration path for the stable BigQuery batch surface. Keep Cloud Run Jobs, Composer DAGs and BigQuery scheduled-query runners outside the stable claim until they have separate real-account evidence.

required_before_promotion: Validate Cloud Run Jobs, Composer DAGs or BigQuery scheduled queries separately before claiming those orchestration surfaces.
Keep full automatic type-widening or type-mutation enforcement in the schema-policy gate instead of the deployment-runner gate.
Keep advanced write modes and streaming provider parity in their dedicated gates.
acceptance: Stable-final GCP claims include the adapter-owned Google Workflows project runner for the documented BigQuery batch surface. Other GCP orchestration surfaces remain explicit non-claims.
